description_long: This irregularly shaped stone features a reddish-brown coloration with a rough, natural texture and a prominent central hole or depression. The surface is uneven, with jagged edges and some areas covered in patches of green moss or lichen, suggesting exposure to moisture and the elements. One side of the stone displays a twisted, root-like protrusion embedded into the rock, adding to its organic appearance. The stone's form is asymmetrical, with a thin, tapering edge on one side and a broader, thicker section on the other. The weathered surface shows signs of erosion and natural wear, with small crevices and pitted areas. This stone would likely be found in an outdoor urban park or garden, serving as a natural obstacle or decorative feature.
description: A reddish-brown, irregular stone with a central hole, rough texture, and patches of moss or lichen. Features a root-like protrusion and weathered, jagged edges.
description_view_0: From this view, the central hole and root-like protrusion are most visible, along with the mossy patches and the broad, uneven surface.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the stone's thin, tapering edge and the side profile of the root-like feature, with less emphasis on the central hole.
description_view_2: The stone appears elongated and flat from this perspective, with the central depression and mossy areas visible along the length.
description_view_3: This view shows the jagged, irregular edge and the back side of the root-like protrusion, with less moss visible.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling with patches of moss and a natural, pitted surface; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough with visible moss and lichen, showing signs of natural erosion and surface pitting.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Partially covered by vegetation, blending naturally with soil and other landscape elements.
